mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term shows zero marginal predictive power with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat during high-inflation low-rate periods, or the mechanism shows negative Rank IC in regimes where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production signal strengthening manufacturing output that supports near-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 consistently across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A single-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50000 in a single month
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of months where PAYEMS drops by more than 50000, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative or flat 60-day returns when both series expand, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production during periods of rising unemployment indicate supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.02 when UNRATE is simultaneously rising.
